label: RAND-36
citation: Hays RD, Sherbourne CD, Mazel RM. (1993). The RAND 36-Item Health Survey 1.0. Health Econ. 2(3):217–227. https://doi.org/10.1002/hec.4730020305
license: Free with a required credit line acknowledging development at RAND as part of the Medical Outcomes Study. RAND grant: "No further written permission is needed for use of this Health Survey."
description: A 36-item patient-reported survey measuring 8 health domains; the freely-licensed sibling of the SF-36 (identical items, RAND scoring). SCAFFOLD: the item set is not yet ingested — no content claim until it lands.
scoring: Scores 0–100 per subscale; higher = better health. RAND scoring differs from the licensed SF-36 scoring.
